title: Preamble transmissions for a random access procedure
abstract: A method may include receiving, by a wireless device, one or more configuration parameters indicating a synchronization signal block (SSB). The method may also include determining, based on a received signal power of the SSB, a number of repetitions of a preamble for a random access procedure. The method may further include determining a gap between a first symbol of a first repetition, of the number of repetitions, of the preamble in a first slot and a second symbol of a second repetition, of the number of repetitions, of the preamble in a second slot to be less than a first number of symbols. The method may additionally include foregoing, skipping, omitting, or otherwise not transmitting the second repetition of the preamble based on determining the gap to be less than the first number of symbols.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: receiving, by a wireless device, one or more configuration parameters indicating a synchronization signal block (SSB); determining, based on a received signal power of the SSB, a number of repetitions of a preamble for a random access procedure; determining a gap between a first symbol of a first repetition, of the number of repetitions, of the preamble in a first slot and a second symbol of a second repetition, of the number of repetitions, of the preamble in a second slot to be less than a first number of symbols; and not transmitting the second repetition of the preamble based on determining the gap to be less than the first number of symbols. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the number is greater than one. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein not transmitting the second repetition of the preamble is in the second slot. 4. The method of any one of claims 1 to 3, preamble is for the random access procedure of a cell. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, further comprising transmitting, in a first slot, a repetition of the preamble. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein determining the number of repetitions is further based on a comparison of the received signal power with one or more power thresholds. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the one or more configuration parameters indicate the one or more power thresholds. 8. The method of any one of claims 5 to 7, wherein the transmitting is via physical random access channel (PRACH). 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein each repetition, of the number of repetitions, of the preamble is associated with a same preamble index. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the first symbol is located first in time among a plurality of symbols associated with the first repetition of the preamble. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the first symbol is located last in time among a plurality of symbols associated with the first repetition of the preamble. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the second symbol is located first in time among a plurality of symbols associated with the second repetition of the preamble. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the second symbol is located last in time among a plurality of symbols associated with the second repetition of the preamble. 14. The method of any one of claims 1 to 13, further comprising determining a set of physical random access channel (PRACH) occasions for transmitting the number of repetitions. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the set of PRACH occasions comprises a number of valid PRACH occasions. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein each PRACH occasion, of the set of PRACH occasions, is associated with the SSB. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the one or more configuration parameters indicate association between the SSB and each PRACH occasion of the set of PRACH occasions. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the gap is based on a sub-carrier spacing configuration. 19. The method of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the first number of symbols is based on a sub-carrier spacing (SCS) configuration. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the first number of symbols is based on an SCS configuration of the preamble. 21. The method of claim 19 or claim 20, wherein the first number of symbols is further based on a smaller SCS configuration between an SCS configuration for the first repetition of the preamble and the SCS configuration for the second repetition of the preamble. 22. The method of claim 21 , wherein the first number of symbols is one of: two; four; sixteen; or thirty two. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the first number of symbols is one of: two based on the smaller SCS configuration being equal to zero or one; four based on the smaller SCS configuration being equal to three; sixteen based on the smaller SCS configuration being equal to five; and thirty two based on the smaller SCS configuration being equal to six. 24. The method of any one of claims 19 to 23, wherein the first number of symbols is one of: two based on an SCS configuration of the preamble being equal to zero or one; four based on an SCS configuration of the preamble being equal to three; an SCS configuration of the preamble being equal to five; and an SCS configuration of the preamble being equal to six. 25. A method comprising: receiving, by a wireless device, one or more configuration parameters indicating a plurality of random-access (RA) channel occasions (ROs) in at least two time slots; transmitting, via the plurality of ROs, a plurality of RA preamble repetitions; determining an RA-radio network temporary identifier (RNTI) based on an RO, of the plurality of ROs, that: ou 

 occurs in a last time slot of the at least two time slots; and is associated with a lowest index in frequency domain in the last time slot; and receiving, based on the RA-RNTI, a random-access response corresponding to the plurality of RA preamble repetitions. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the one or more configuration parameters are: one or more radio resource control configuration parameters; or one or more system information parameters. 27. The method of claim 25 or 26, wherein the plurality of ROs comprise: one or more first ROs; and a second plurality of ROs. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the one or more first ROs is different from the second plurality of ROs. 29. The method of claim 27 or 28, wherein each RO, of the one or more first ROs, is different from each/any RO, of the second plurality of ROs. 30. The method of any one of claims 27 to 29, wherein the each RO, of the one or more first ROs, occurs/is located at a first time slot of the at least two time slots. 31. The method of any one of claims 27 to 30, wherein each RO, of the second plurality of ROs, occurs/is located at the last time slot. 32. The method of any one of claims 27 to 31 , wherein the second plurality of ROs comprises the RO. 33. The method of any one of claims 25 to 32, wherein the transmitting comprises transmitting each RA preamble repetition, of the plurality of RA preamble repetitions, via a respective RO of the plurality of ROs. 34. The method of any one of claims 25 to 33, further comprising determining a second RA-RNTI based on a second RO, of the plurality of ROs, that: occurs in a last time slot of the at least two time slots; and is associated with a highest index in the frequency domain in the last time slot. 35. The method of claim 34, further comprising receiving, based on the second RA-RNTI, a second random-access response corresponding to the plurality of RA preamble repetitions. 36. A method comprising: transmitting, to a wireless device, one or more configuration parameters indicating a synchronization signal block (SSB), wherein the wireless device determines, based on a received signal power of the SSB, a number of repetitions of a preamble for a random access procedure, and wherein the wireless device determines a gap between a first symbol of a first repetition, of the number of repetitions, of the preamble in a first slot and a second symbol of a second repetition, of the number of repetitions, of the preamble in a second slot to be less than a first number of symbols; and 

 not receiving the second repetition of the preamble based on the gap being determined to be less than the first number of symbols. 37. A method comprising: transmitting, to a wireless device, one or more configuration parameters indicating a plurality of randomaccess (RA) channel occasions (ROs) in at least two time slots; receiving, via the plurality of ROs, a plurality of RA preamble repetitions, wherein an RA-radio network temporary identifier (RNTI) is determined based on an RO, of the plurality of ROs, that: occurs in a last time slot of the at least two time slots; and is associated with a lowest index in frequency domain in the last time slot; and transmitting, based on the RA-RNTI, a random-access response corresponding to the plurality of RA preamble repetitions. 38. An apparatus comprising one or more processors and memory storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the apparatus at least to perform the method according to any one of claims 1-37. 39. A non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a device, cause the device to perform the method according to any one of claims 1-37. 40. An apparatus comprising circuitry configured to perform the method according to any one of claims 1 -37. 41. A computer program product encoding instructions for performing the method according to any one of claims 1 - 37.
